what_it_does: A dsCard demo/gallery page that renders the ConceptV slide-archetype system. It imports React (UMD via unpkg) and the compiled design-system bundle, then uses the exported Slide component to build slides from pure content objects. Three sections: one split spec rendered at three LOD levels (summary/pitch/full), a hand-listed archetype gallery (cover, metric-band, compare, triptych, stepper, diagram·hub), and a capital-raise archetype gallery that pulls each type's default content from a single-source catalogue (CV_ARCHETYPE_CATALOG/META).
observations: Top-line HTML comment is a @dsCard annotation with group/name/subtitle/viewport metadata (1280x2600).
Loads React 18.3.1 and ReactDOM from unpkg with SRI integrity hashes and crossorigin.
Pulls the namespace from window.ConceptVDesignSystem_c8f43c and destructures Slide from it.
frameInto() helper creates a scaled artboard: renders at DESIGN_W=1100 then scales to fit the frame width via transform and a ResizeObserver + requestAnimationFrame + setTimeout(350) fit routine.
Each Slide is rendered with archetype + content + lod + surface:'slide' + density:'compact'.
splitContent demonstrates content-as-data: bullets carry title/text/bullet/priority/detail so LOD pruning can drop 'support' detail by priority.
Gallery cases feed real ConceptV content (Virtual Hubs, 70+ delivered, $1.5M+ IP, 41,500+ users).
diagram case hands a hub graph (center Vi, diamond/circle/hex/octagon node shapes) to the graph solver.
Capital-raise section reads defaultsOf(key) from CV_ARCHETYPE_CATALOG (id 'surface.deck-slide.'+key) so card and registry can't drift; falls back to {} which the builder tolerates.
twelve new capital-raise keys incl. five diagram sub-types: orbital, stacked, spectrum, manifold, fidelity.
standouts: Stated thesis: a slide archetype is a pure function content → CVNode tree; design = f(content, axisPosition).
LOD is recomputation (prune by narrative priority, grow sections/atom depth) not a different layout.
Capital-raise content is pulled from the single-source catalogue explicitly to prevent card/registry drift.
conventions: Follows the design-system's single-source rule: content from CV_ARCHETYPE_CATALOG, look from --tokens, no hand layout.
Uses var(--token) throughout the inline <style> (zone/ink/accent/font/elev/fs tokens).
@dsCard comment convention so the gallery machinery can catalogue this page.
Inline scaling/fit pattern via ResizeObserver rather than a CSS-only approach.
concerns: Hardcoded fallback colours in inline style (#fff via var fallback) and the c8f43c namespace suffix are magic strings local to this page.
External unpkg React load means the page depends on network/CDN to render at all.
fit() relies on a setTimeout(350) fallback, a timing heuristic that can misfire if render is slow.
notes: A self-describing demo page proving slides are pure content→tree builders recomputed across LOD, with the capital-raise archetypes sourced from the single catalogue to avoid drift.
questions: Where is the Slide component and the block/graph solvers actually implemented (referenced via the bundle)?
What populates window.CV_ARCHETYPE_CATALOG / CV_ARCHETYPE_META at runtime?
Is the c8f43c namespace suffix stable across bundle rebuilds or regenerated?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the @dsCard subtitle promises 25 corpus archetypes as content→tree builders recomputed across LOD, and the page renders exactly that across three galleries.
intention_signals: Demonstrate and visually prove the generative slide system: that one spec recomputes across LOD and that every archetype (including the mined capital-raise grammar) is a builder fed registry-sourced content, so the eye can verify the engine works.
novelty: The explicit framing of LOD as recomputation by narrative priority, and the five new diagram sub-types (orbital verb-ring, stacked pipeline, 1-D spectrum axis, converging manifold, progressive-fidelity stepper) as first-class content→tree builders.
contribution: Contributes a live, self-scaling visual demonstration of the slide-archetype generative engine and a worked example of sourcing archetype defaults from the single catalogue, plus the enumerated capital-raise archetype grammar and diagram sub-types.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML dsCard gallery rendering ConceptV slide archetypes (cover, compare, triptych, diagram, capital-raise set) via the Slide component as pure content→CVNode-tree builders recomputed across three LOD levels.
intention_for_embedding: To visually prove the slide system where design = f(content, axis), LOD is recomputation, and archetype defaults come from the single-source catalogue to prevent drift.
